title: MRD And Relapse Measurement Glossary v0

terms.label: Measurable residual disease
terms.definition: A sensitive measurement frame for residual myeloma after treatment response, also called minimal residual disease in many sources.
measurement_context: Requires method, specimen, threshold, and timing before comparison.
May refer to marrow-based assays, imaging-linked response categories, or study-specific endpoint definitions.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use as the umbrella term for highly sensitive residual disease measurement.
Record the source's exact terminology when it uses minimal residual disease instead.
what_not_to_infer: Do not treat MRD status as a cure claim.
Do not treat an MRD label as comparable across studies without method context.
limitations: MRD meaning depends on assay, threshold, specimen quality, timing, and disease distribution.

terms.label: MRD negativity
terms.definition: A source-defined state in which residual clonal plasma cells are not detected by a specified MRD method at a specified threshold and time point.
measurement_context: Pair with assay method, sensitivity threshold, specimen source, time point, and response category.
The IMWG consensus criteria include marrow MRD categories with a minimum sensitivity of 10^-5 or better.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use only with the method and threshold that produced the result.
Separate single time-point negativity from sustained negativity.
what_not_to_infer: Do not read negative as no disease exists anywhere.
Do not claim cure from MRD negativity alone.
limitations: Sampling and assay sensitivity can produce false reassurance if context is missing.

terms.label: MRD sensitivity threshold
terms.definition: The stated detection sensitivity or limit used to interpret an MRD result, such as 10^-5 when the source specifies it.
measurement_context: Record the threshold separately from the method name.
Thresholds should be source-stated, not inferred from a general assay family.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use for comparing whether two MRD claims were measured at similar depth.
Flag MRD claims with missing or ambiguous threshold.
what_not_to_infer: Do not assume every study used 10^-5.
Do not assume a lower numerical detection limit makes results clinically interchangeable.
limitations: Laboratory validation, sample quality, and analysis approach still matter even when thresholds match.

terms.label: Bone marrow MRD
terms.definition: MRD assessment performed on a bone marrow aspirate or related marrow sample.
measurement_context: Record aspirate source, adequacy, method, threshold, and timing.
Interpret with caution when disease may be patchy or extramedullary.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use as the specimen context for marrow-based flow or sequencing MRD claims.
Capture sample timing relative to therapy.
what_not_to_infer: Do not assume marrow MRD captures every disease site.
Do not compare marrow MRD with imaging endpoints without saying they measure different contexts.
limitations: Marrow sampling can miss spatially uneven disease or extramedullary disease.

terms.label: Flow cytometry MRD
terms.definition: MRD assessment using flow cytometry to identify phenotypically aberrant clonal plasma cells.
measurement_context: Record whether next-generation flow or another validated method was used.
Record analysis approach, sensitivity, and sample adequacy when available.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use for flow-based MRD claims and extraction fields.
Prefer source-stated method details before cross-study comparison.
what_not_to_infer: Do not compare flow results across studies without harmonization context.
Do not assume a generic flow label identifies the exact sensitivity.
limitations: Panel design, acquisition depth, analysis strategy, and specimen quality affect interpretation.

terms.label: Sequencing MRD
terms.definition: MRD assessment using sequencing-based detection of clonal plasma-cell markers in a sample.
measurement_context: Record platform or validated equivalent and the stated sensitivity threshold.
Record specimen and timing alongside the sequencing result.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use for sequencing-based MRD claims and extraction fields.
Separate sequencing MRD from flow MRD unless a source explicitly compares them.
what_not_to_infer: Do not treat sequencing and flow as interchangeable without context.
Do not infer clinical actionability from platform name alone.
limitations: Sequencing results depend on assay validation, baseline clone identification, sample quality, and threshold.

terms.label: Imaging plus MRD
terms.definition: A response-assessment context that combines marrow MRD negativity with imaging response criteria.
measurement_context: Record imaging modality, baseline or prior imaging context, and marrow MRD method.
Use when the source explicitly combines marrow MRD and imaging response.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use to separate marrow-only MRD claims from marrow plus imaging response claims.
Flag missing imaging context in relapse or durable-remission comparisons.
what_not_to_infer: Do not use marrow MRD alone to rule out extramedullary disease.
Do not assume imaging methods are comparable without source details.
limitations: Imaging interpretation depends on modality, timing, baseline lesions, and response criteria.

terms.label: Sustained MRD negativity
terms.definition: MRD negativity confirmed over a specified interval rather than at a single time point.
measurement_context: The IMWG consensus criteria define sustained MRD negativity using confirmation at least 1 year apart.
Record the exact duration and whether later evaluations extend the duration claim.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use only when repeat MRD-negative assessments and interval are source-supported.
Keep this distinct from a single MRD-negative assessment.
what_not_to_infer: Do not collapse one MRD-negative time point into sustained negativity.
Do not claim cure from sustained MRD negativity without stronger evidence and review.
limitations: Sustained status depends on repeat testing, time interval, and continued follow-up.

terms.label: Complete response
terms.definition: A conventional response category defined by the source's multiple myeloma response criteria.
measurement_context: Record the exact criteria set used by the source.
Keep separate from MRD-negative categories.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use for source-defined response category extraction.
Treat as a prerequisite or context only when the source criteria say so.
what_not_to_infer: Do not equate complete response with MRD negativity.
Do not equate complete response with cure.
limitations: Response category meaning depends on the criteria version, assay context, and disease-state setting.

terms.label: Stringent complete response
terms.definition: A source-defined response category that adds stricter criteria beyond complete response.
measurement_context: Record the source's criteria version and fields used.
Keep separate from MRD negativity unless the source explicitly links them.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use when a source reports stringent complete response as a response category.
Record whether MRD was assessed separately.
what_not_to_infer: Do not treat stringent complete response as deeper than MRD without MRD data.
Do not claim cure from stringent complete response.
limitations: Different sources may report response categories without enough measurement detail for direct comparison.

terms.label: Progression-free survival
terms.definition: A study endpoint measuring time until disease progression or death according to the source's endpoint definition.
measurement_context: Record population, disease state, line of therapy, follow-up duration, and censoring context when available.
Use alongside MRD status only when the source reports their association or endpoint analysis.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use as a study-level endpoint for public evidence claims.
Preserve follow-up and disease-state scope.
what_not_to_infer: Do not interpret as patient-specific prognosis.
Do not compare progression-free survival across studies without population and follow-up context.
limitations: Endpoint definitions, censoring, and follow-up duration can change interpretation.

terms.label: Overall survival
terms.definition: A study endpoint measuring survival according to the source's endpoint definition and follow-up.
measurement_context: Record population, disease state, follow-up duration, and analysis context.
Use with MRD associations only when the source reports the relationship.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use as a population-level study endpoint.
Keep endpoint context visible in any claim set.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer individual survival.
Do not treat association with MRD status as proof that changing treatment based on MRD improves survival.
limitations: Overall survival can be affected by many later therapies and non-study factors.

terms.label: Relapse or disease progression
terms.definition: A source-defined event in which disease returns or progresses after response or during follow-up.
measurement_context: Record the source's progression or relapse definition.
Record biomarker, imaging, clinical, or endpoint triggers when available.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use when mapping relapse events, progression endpoints, or post-response failure.
Require source-defined event criteria before comparing relapse claims.
what_not_to_infer: Do not compare relapse claims across studies unless event definitions align.
Do not treat any one measurement trigger as sufficient for patient-specific interpretation.
limitations: Relapse and progression definitions vary by source, study context, and available measurements.

terms.label: Bone marrow target status
terms.definition: A source-reported assessment of target expression or target absence in bone marrow plasma cells or tumor sample context.
measurement_context: Record target name, specimen source, assay method, timing, prior target exposure, and response context.
For BCMA-directed therapy maps, separate marrow target status from soluble biomarker and sequencing signals.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use when a relapse map depends on whether a target was present, low, or absent in a specimen.
Require source-stated assay and specimen context before comparing sequential anti-target strategies.
what_not_to_infer: Do not treat one marrow target-status result as a full-body disease map.
Do not use target status alone as treatment guidance.
limitations: Assay method, specimen adequacy, disease patchiness, and timing can change interpretation.

terms.label: Antigen density
terms.definition: A source-reported quantitative or semiquantitative target-abundance measurement on tumor cells or model systems.
measurement_context: Record target name, density unit or scoring method, specimen or model context, and comparator target when available.
Use separate fields for tumor expression and normal-tissue expression.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use for alternate-target research maps where target abundance is part of the evidence.
Preserve whether the evidence is clinical, translational, or preclinical.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer clinical efficacy from target density.
Do not infer safety from tumor expression without normal-tissue context.
limitations: Target-density methods and thresholds may not be comparable across sources.

terms.label: Soluble BCMA
terms.definition: A source-reported blood measurement of soluble BCMA used as candidate context for BCMA target biology.
measurement_context: Record blood assay, timing, paired marrow target status, and therapy sequence when available.
Use as a candidate measurement field rather than a standalone rule.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use when a public extraction preserves soluble BCMA alongside marrow BCMA expression.
Flag missing paired specimen context.
what_not_to_infer: Do not use soluble BCMA alone for patient-specific interpretation.
Do not assume soluble BCMA substitutes for direct target assessment.
limitations: Assay thresholds, timing, and paired marrow context require source-specific review.

terms.label: Normal-tissue reactivity screen
terms.definition: A source-reported safety-context assay or screen that assesses candidate target reactivity outside tumor cells.
measurement_context: Record target, model system, tissue panel, assay method, and clinical translation status.
Use especially when alternate-target immunotherapy evidence is preclinical.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use as a minimum measurement dependency for alternate-target claims.
Keep tumor-target expression separate from normal-tissue safety context.
what_not_to_infer: Do not treat a preclinical screen as proof of patient safety.
Do not infer clinical readiness without protocol and regulatory context.
limitations: Preclinical safety-context assays do not replace clinical safety evidence.

terms.label: Blood mass spectrometry MRD
terms.definition: A source-defined peripheral blood or serum residual-disease measurement context using mass spectrometry methods.
measurement_context: Record assay family, peripheral blood or serum context, threshold or detection-status wording, timing, cohort, and paired marrow context when source-defined.
Use as complementary residual-disease source context, not as a public substitute for marrow MRD.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use when a public source reports blood-based mass spectrometry residual-disease context.
Keep the assay family and limitation note visible before downstream reuse.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer substitution for marrow MRD.
Do not infer prognosis, monitoring action, treatment adaptation, response status, or patient-specific meaning.
limitations: Blood mass spectrometry context depends on assay method, timing, threshold or detection status, cohort, and paired-modality context.

terms.label: MRD/PET-CT modality discordance
terms.definition: A source-defined paired-modality state where marrow MRD and PET-CT or imaging residual-disease context do not collapse into a single global disease flag.
measurement_context: Record paired marrow MRD method, threshold, imaging modality, timing alignment, cohort, endpoint role, and source-defined category.
Keep marrow-positive/PET-negative and marrow-negative/PET-positive categories distinct when a source defines them.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use to preserve visible cross-modality discordance and missingness.
Route downstream output to refusal or source-context-needed states when a user asks for patient meaning.
what_not_to_infer: Do not interpret images or lesion sites.
Do not infer prognosis, monitoring action, response status, treatment adaptation, modality ranking, or patient-specific meaning.
limitations: Discordance meaning depends on study design, modality timing, marrow method, threshold, imaging criteria, and cohort context.

terms.label: Spatial marrow architecture context
terms.definition: A research context for spatial transcriptomics, trephine biopsy, marrow architecture, cellular niches, and site-limited sampling in myeloma.
measurement_context: Record biopsy/specimen context, spatial method, cell or niche context, cohort scope, site/sampling limitation, and method boundary.
Use missing spatial state as visible missingness, not as absence of spatially restricted or extramedullary disease.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use for source-level marrow architecture and microenvironment context.
Keep spatial context separate from imaging, marrow aspirate MRD, and blood assay context.
what_not_to_infer: Do not interpret a biopsy, image, or site-specific disease state for a person.
Do not infer prognosis, treatment selection, target selection, or patient-specific resistance status.
limitations: Spatial context is method-, specimen-, site-, and cohort-scoped and may be absent from otherwise multimodal records.

terms.label: Assay/specimen quality
terms.definition: A required source-context field family for method lineage, threshold status, sample adequacy, specimen quality, timing, paired-modality context, imaging criteria, spatial sampling, host-context separation, and private-review boundaries before residual-disease comparison.
measurement_context: Record method, threshold, sample or specimen context, timing alignment, paired-modality context, duration/follow-up, and not-reported states before comparison.

Use the assay/specimen quality failure-mode checklist to route missing method, threshold, specimen, timing, paired modality, imaging, spatial, host-context, private-review, and modality-collapse states.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use to block comparison when method, threshold, specimen, paired modality, imaging, spatial, or timing context is missing.
Expose assay-quality missingness as a limitation before downstream reuse.
Route real-report or lab-validity review requests to private_lab_or_clinical_review_needed.
what_not_to_infer: Do not compare modalities when quality, threshold, specimen, paired context, imaging criteria, spatial sampling, or timing context is missing.
Do not infer response status, prognosis, monitoring action, lab validity, or clinical decision.
limitations: Quality and timing fields are source-context fields only and do not establish lab validity for a person.

terms.label: Paired-modality timing context
terms.definition: A source-defined timing relationship between residual-disease modalities that must be visible before discordance language or comparison is reused.
measurement_context: Record whether modalities were assessed at the same source-defined timepoint, within a reported window, or in incompatible or missing timing buckets.
Use timepoint_alignment_needed when paired assessment timing is absent or not comparable enough for the requested public-source comparison.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use before describing cross-modality concordance or discordance.
Use as a fail-closed timing state for future synthetic refusal fixtures.
what_not_to_infer: Do not treat paired results as comparable when assessment timing is missing, incompatible, or not source-defined.
Do not infer durability, prognosis, monitoring action, treatment action, or patient-specific meaning from timing context.
limitations: Timing context is a source-scoped representation field and does not establish clinical comparability or endpoint validity.

terms.label: Residual-disease modality discordance
terms.definition: A public-safe uncertainty state for source-scoped residual-disease signals that are measured by different modalities or have missing paired-modality context.
measurement_context: Record modality family, method, specimen, threshold or detection status, timing, paired-state context, source IDs, and limitation notes.
Expose discordance or missingness as a refusal reason instead of converting it into a global disease-state claim.
use_in_public_artifacts: Use as the explicit state when residual-disease modalities disagree or are incomplete.
Route model-output and validator behavior to visible uncertainty, source-context-needed, or refusal states.
what_not_to_infer: Do not collapse one modality into a global disease status.
Do not infer prognosis, treatment response, monitoring action, modality ranking, or patient-specific meaning.
limitations: Discordance is a representation boundary, not a clinical interpretation or evidence grade.

review_notes: Built from NCI PDQ context plus PubMed-indexed IMWG response criteria, MRD survival meta-analysis, and flow-cytometry harmonization sources.
Designed as a public measurement guardrail for later durable-remission, relapse, and claim-set artifacts.

Updated with residual-disease modality-discordance terms for blood mass spectrometry MRD, MRD/PET-CT discordance, spatial marrow architecture, assay/specimen quality, and fail-closed modality-discordance handling.

Updated with assay/specimen quality failure-mode checklist and paired-modality timing context so missing method, threshold, specimen quality, timing, paired modality, imaging, spatial, host-context, private-review, and modality-collapse states remain explicit before comparison.
